import io
import list
import string

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut cur = 0
  mut sign = 1
  mut has = false
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if b >= 48 and b <= 57
        cur = cur * 10 + (b - 48)
        has = true
      else if b == 45 and not has
        sign = 0 - 1
      else
        if has
          result = list.push(result, cur * sign)
          cur = 0
          sign = 1
          has = false
        end
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    result = list.push(result, cur * sign)
  end
  result
end

fn bsearch(_ xs: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = xs.len()
  while lo < hi
    let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
    match xs.get(mid)
    when Some(v)
      if v == t
        return Some(mid)
      else if v < t
        lo = mid + 1
      else
        hi = mid
      end
    when None
      return None
    end
  end
  None
end

fn main() -> Unit
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(line1)
    match io.read_line()
    when Some(line2)
      let xs = parse_ints(line1)
      let trimmed = string.trim(line2)
      match string.parse_int(trimmed)
      when Some(t)
        match bsearch(xs, t)
        when Some(i)
          println("index=#{i}")
        when None
          println("not_found")
        end
      when None
        println("not_found")
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
  when None
    ()
  end
end
